_notes._about: This is the shape of the config the interactive prompt saves to ~/.canvas-archive/config.json and loads automatically on the next run. You normally never edit this by hand: run the tool with no arguments and answer the prompts. Anything passed on the command line overrides the saved value. There is no --config flag; this example file is documentation only. The tool reads ~/.canvas-archive/config.json, not this file.
_notes.base_url: Root of your Canvas instance. FFT is https://canvas.instructure.com. Institutional is usually https://<school>.instructure.com.
_notes.cookies: Path to a Cookie-Editor JSON export. ~ is expanded. Re-export when cookies expire (you will see 401s).
_notes.output_root: Folder where each <Course Name (semester) YYYY>/ folder gets written.
_notes.skip_files: Skip downloading the Files section entirely.
_notes.max_file_size_mb: Skip any single file larger than this many MB.
_notes.include_unpublished: Include unpublished assignments, pages, modules, discussions, quizzes, and files.
_notes.zip: Also produce a .zip of each course folder.
_notes.term_scheme: How the academic term in folder names is recognized from a course's earliest assignment due date. One of: korean (default; Spring=1 Feb-Jul, Fall=2 Aug-Dec, January counts as the prior year's Fall), us (Spring=1 Jan-May, Summer=2 Jun-Jul, Fall=3 Aug-Dec), trimester (1 Jan-Apr, 2 May-Aug, 3 Sep-Dec), summer_winter (Winter=1 Dec-Feb, Summer=2 Jun-Aug, regular=3 otherwise), or none (do not add a term marker). Folders are named '<Course Name> (<term>) <year>'.
_notes.scope: Course scope is chosen at run time with --course-id <id> or --all (or the interactive prompt), not stored in this file.
